qa.overall_assessment: Single-line doc-only replacement verified. Authoritative regex is present in Edge Cases section, stale form is absent (Python fixed-string check), both sections and the hook _BULK_CANONICAL_RE are byte-for-byte identical, and the $ anchor correctly rejects forged-argument suffixes (confirmed by Codex live bash test).

out_of_scope_observations.observation: Technical Hints section contains grep-qE/grep-qP examples that are inconsistent with the bash [[ =~ ]] recommendation at line 201 — pre-existing inconsistency noted in dev report qa_notes; out of scope for this MICRO doc-only fix

Historical/evidence artifacts still contain the stale regex as quoted material (as part of documenting what was fixed). These are read-only evidence files; stale regex is not in the target file's Edge Cases section. Out of scope per BA spec.

AC1.evidence: Python fixed-string substring match (no regex interpretation) scoped to ## Edge Cases & Risks section (lines 116-122): stale optional-double-venv-group literal '(source[[:space:]]+venv/bin/activate[[:space:]]*&&[[:space:]]*)?' absent (False); authoritative regex literal '^source[[:space:]]+venv/bin/activate[[:space:]]*&&[[:space:]]*python3?[[:space:]]+/root/\.claude/scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel\.py$' present (True). grep count for '(source[[:space:]]' in full file = 0.

AC2.evidence: Python byte-for-byte comparison of all three locations: hook _BULK_CANONICAL_RE (hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh:677), Edge Cases line 120 (backtick-extracted), Technical Hints line 197 (stripped of leading whitespace). All three equal '^source[[:space:]]+venv/bin/activate[[:space:]]*&&[[:space:]]*python3?[[:space:]]+/root/\.claude/scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel\.py$'. hook==EC: True, hook==TH: True, all three identical: True. git diff shows Technical Hints section (context lines 188-212) has zero changed lines.

AC3.evidence: git diff --numstat HEAD -- docs/dev/ticket-20260526-052559.md shows 1 1 (1 insertion, 1 deletion). git diff --unified=0 shows @@ -120 +120 @@ — exactly one line changed. Unscoped git diff --name-status HEAD shows 5 modified files; all 4 other modified files (commands/INDEX.md, commands/close.md, docs/dev/INDEX.md, logs/lifecycle.jsonl) are pre-existing baseline dirty entries documented in dev-report.baseline_dirty_snapshot and thus excluded from this task's scope per QA baseline contract. Task-scoped diff: 1 file, 1 line, section unchanged outside the Critical regression risk bullet.

AC4.evidence: By inspection: authoritative regex ends with '\.py$'; test string 'source venv/bin/activate && python3 /root/.claude/scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py --sid FORGED' has ' --sid FORGED' after .py which does not satisfy the $ end anchor. Additionally, Codex ran live bash [[ =~ ]] tests during adversarial review and confirmed: canonical MATCH, forged NO, doublevenv NO, badroot NO, newlineprefix NO. Positive match also confirmed: 'source venv/bin/activate && python /root/.claude/scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py' (commands/commit.md:81 form) MATCH.

codex_findings_disposition.issue: AC3 is under-scoped: scoping diff to one file does not prove repo has exactly one changed file
codex_findings_disposition.action: Re-ran git diff --name-status HEAD (unscoped). 5 files appear; 4 are in baseline_dirty_snapshot. Evidence added to AC3 entry confirming task-scoped single-file constraint holds.

codex_findings_disposition.issue: AC1 grep evidence may be unreliable if it used pattern matching instead of fixed-string matching
codex_findings_disposition.action: Re-ran AC1 check using Python substring/fixed-string match. Stale form absent, authoritative form present. Result unambiguous.

codex_findings_disposition.issue: Missing direct comparison to _BULK_CANONICAL_RE in hook
codex_findings_disposition.action: Extracted _BULK_CANONICAL_RE from hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh:677 and byte-compared to both line 120 and line 197. All three identical.

codex_findings_disposition.issue: Positive-match side under-verified: should confirm canonical command still matches
codex_findings_disposition.action: Codex ran live bash [[ =~ ]] test during review: 'canonical MATCH'. Evidence cited in AC4.

codex_findings_disposition.issue: OBSERVATION_ONLY: AC4 inspection-only is acceptable if spec says inspection-only
codex_findings_disposition.action: No action needed. AC spec explicitly states verification is inspection-only.

All four Codex issues were addressed with additional verification steps. No blocking issues found.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex raised 4 findings: (1) AC3 under-scoped — scoping diff to one file does not prove repo has exactly one changed file; (2) AC1 grep evidence may be unreliable with pattern matching; (3) missing direct comparison to hook _BULK_CANONICAL_RE; (4) positive-match side under-verified for canonical command. All 4 addressed with additional verification. One OBSERVATION_ONLY finding: AC4 inspection-only is acceptable per spec.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Re-ran unscoped git diff (AC3); re-ran AC1 with Python fixed-string matching; extracted and byte-compared hook _BULK_CANONICAL_RE to both sections (all three identical); cited Codex live bash test output for AC4 positive/negative match confirmation.
